name: Incline Dumbbell Curls
description: Targets the long head of the biceps for peak development. Helps improve arm symmetry and strength.
steps: Sit on incline bench\nCurl dumbbells upward\nLower slowly

name: Drag Curl
description: Focuses on biceps with minimal shoulder involvement. Great for strict form and isolation.
steps: Keep bar close to body\nPull elbows back\nCurl upward

name: Narrow Grip Barbell Curl
description: Emphasizes the outer biceps head. Builds thickness and definition.
steps: Grip bar narrow\nCurl up slowly\nControl the descent

name: Wide Grip Barbell Curl
description: Targets inner biceps for fuller look. Improves arm width.
steps: Grip wider than shoulders\nCurl bar up\nLower with control

name: Preacher Curls
description: Strict isolation exercise reducing momentum. Excellent for peak contraction.
steps: Place arms on pad\nCurl up\nLower fully

name: Concentration Curl
description: Highly focused curl for peak biceps contraction. Great for mind-muscle connection.
steps: Sit and rest elbow on thigh\nCurl dumbbell\nLower slowly

name: Hammer Curls
description: Targets brachialis and forearms. Adds arm thickness.

name: Cross Body Hammer Curls
description: Variation focusing on brachialis. Improves arm density.
steps: Curl across body\nSqueeze at top\nLower slowly

name: Rope Hammer Curls
description: Works brachialis with cable tension. Maintains constant resistance.

name: Dumbbell Curls
description: Classic biceps builder. Helps develop size and strength.
steps: Curl both arms\nSqueeze at top\nLower slowly

name: Overhead Dumbbell Extension
description: Targets triceps long head. Builds arm size.
steps: Hold dumbbell overhead\nLower behind head\nExtend up

name: Skull Crushers
description: Effective triceps isolation movement. Builds strength and mass.
steps: Lower bar to forehead\nExtend arms\nKeep elbows fixed

name: Dips (Weighted)
description: Compound triceps movement. Adds strength and size.
steps: Lower body down\nPush up\nKeep torso upright

name: Cable Rope Pushdowns
description: Targets lateral head of triceps. Great for definition.
steps: Push rope down\nSpread at bottom\nReturn slowly

name: JM Press
description: Hybrid press for triceps. Combines bench press and skull crusher.
steps: Lower bar toward chest\nPress up\nKeep elbows tight

name: Reverse Grip Cable Pushdowns
description: Targets medial head of triceps. Improves arm detail.

name: Diamond Pushups
description: Bodyweight triceps builder. Engages chest and core.

name: Reverse Grip Bench Press
description: Focuses on triceps. Reduces chest involvement.
steps: Grip bar reverse\nLower to chest\nPress up

name: Barbell Overhead Press
description: Builds shoulder strength. Targets front delts.

name: Dumbbell Shoulder Press
description: Improves shoulder stability. Builds strength evenly.
steps: Press dumbbells up\nLower controlled\nKeep core tight

name: Front Raise
description: Isolates front delts. Enhances shoulder definition.

name: Machine Shoulder Press
description: Controlled shoulder press. Good for beginners.

name: Dumbbell Lateral Raise
description: Targets side delts. Builds shoulder width.

name: Cable Lateral Raise (Leaning)
description: Maintains tension throughout movement. Improves isolation.

name: Upright Row
description: Targets traps and side delts. Builds upper body strength.
steps: Pull bar upward\nKeep elbows high\nLower slowly

name: Reverse Pec Deck
description: Isolates rear delts. Improves posture.
steps: Pull handles back\nSqueeze shoulder blades\nReturn slowly

name: Face Pulls
description: Strengthens rear delts and traps. Improves shoulder health.

name: Bent Over Lateral Raise
description: Targets rear delts. Enhances shoulder balance.

name: Incline Dumbbell Press
description: Targets upper chest. Builds mass and strength.

name: Incline Barbell Press
description: Develops upper chest. Improves pressing strength.

name: Preacher Hammer Curls
description: Targets brachioradialis and upper forearms. Helps build forearm thickness and strength.
steps: Sit on preacher bench\nHold dumbbells neutral\nCurl upward and lower slowly

name: Dumbbell Hammer Curls
description: Builds brachioradialis and forearm size. Improves grip strength.

name: Reverse Barbell Curls
description: Targets upper forearms and improves grip. Builds forearm strength.
steps: Hold bar with overhand grip\nCurl upward\nLower slowly

description: Targets forearm extensors with constant tension. Improves muscle control.

description: Strengthens forearm extensors and grip endurance. Great for overall forearm development.

name: Plate Pinches
description: Improves grip strength and forearm endurance. Engages flexor muscles.

name: Behind-the-Back Wrist Curl
description: Targets lower forearm flexors. Improves wrist strength.

name: Barbell Wrist Curls
description: Isolates forearm flexors. Builds wrist strength and size.
steps: Hold bar underhand\nCurl wrists upward\nLower slowly
